summary: Excellent soundboard recording (crisp but a few cuts) of an electrifying Forum show highlighted by a monster Estimated Prophet/Space/Take‑You‑Home sequence and soaring drums.
review: The GD89‑02‑11 capture is a clean sound‑board source that retains the raw energy of the Inglewood Forum gig – listeners repeatedly praise the crystal‑clear mix, even if Bird Song and a portion of the Drums are edited out. Musically the night shines with an explosive Estimated Prophet segueing into Space and Brent’s lullaby Take You Home, plus fire‑brand performances of China, Rider, Black Peter and Queen Jane. Overall the show is celebrated as one of the band’s most memorable LA dates.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen detailed reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance and sound, with only minor complaints about cuts; this yields a high combined score weighted toward show quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear and well‑balanced mix praised by many reviewers. The only technical drawbacks are the missing segment of Bird Song and an edited-out chant at the start of Drums.

standout_songs: Estimated Prophet > Space > Take You Home
poor_songs: Estimated Prophet (early section perceived as lacking momentum by one reviewer)
"I Will Take You Home" (not a favorite for at least one listener)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is solid and steady, delivering classics like Mississippi and Candyman. The second set erupts with high‑energy numbers, a dramatic Estimated Prophet/Space stretch, and an unforgettable drum segment featuring Airto’s percussion and the chanting duo Flora/Diana.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for his soaring lead lines – “Jerry is really pickin it,” “Jerry’s happy stepping back later but still strong,” and “Jerry’s 89 version of Push” are highlighted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as “nice all‑around work from Phil” with a solid bass foundation throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland receives praise for his lullaby Take You Home and swirling organ textures; no direct comments on Keith or Vince in these reviews.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum segment is called “spectacular,” “the most spectacular thing I’d ever seen and heard,” with Airto’s guest percussion adding a wild, chant‑filled dimension.
